title: Music soothes the savage Eva beast
display_title: Music soothes the savage Eva beast
wikitext:
[]: Music soothes the savage Eva beast...             Quote Post by Olin of Xephon » Thu Aug 26, 2004 12:00 am In back of several of the new rerelease manga, they have a "alternate soundtrack" list. Basically this is a list of songs that represent a epsisode from Eva. Well, The list didn't sound very good to me, but I'm a little weird. Anyway, in the spirit of wasting time, one of our favorite pastimes in this forum, is there any particular pieces of music, rock songs, or even love ballads that put you in mind of a ceratin eva character? Sequence? Episode? Apart fom the obvious that is. ( I.E. Fly me to the moon)

[]:             Quote Post by wildarmsheero » Thu Aug 26, 2004 7:57 am Weezer's Tired of Sex . Don't ask me why... but it makes sense in my head.

[]:             Quote Post by kosure » Thu Aug 26, 2004 8:19 am I know these might be a little obscure for some of you but, for Shinji I like Sigur Ros' Di Do for Rei I like "Ladies and Gentlemen We are Floating In Space" by Spiritualized "Italy" by Fuck and "How to Fight Lonliness" by Wilco Finally for Asuka I Like "Girl Inform Me" by the Shins

[]:             Quote Post by MongolSquad » Thu Aug 26, 2004 9:41 am -Apex Twin's "Let My Fish Loose", the abosolute most depressing song ever. You could be the happiest person on earth and this song will still pull you down. I can see it inserted at appropiate scenes. -Queen's "Bohemian Rhapsody" wouldn't be bad either. -The Seatbelts "Space Lion", I know its from Cowboy Bebop but it has sad, but yet primal feel to it. Like it would be used for after an intense battle when the characters are still coping with some of the consequneces. Or after a special someone passed away.

[]:             Quote Post by Karma Burn » Thu Aug 26, 2004 4:21 pm Could someone be gracious enough to post the soundtrack list? As in what manga volume and what scene goes with each song. "Yeah, Whatever" by Splendor for certain scenes involing Shinji and Asuka. "(Even) More human than human" by the X-ecutioners (feat. Slug from Atmosphere) for EoE when Third Impact starts.

[]:             Quote Post by Genocide » Thu Aug 26, 2004 5:11 pm Metallica's Wasting My Hate because I saw an Eva AMV that featured the song. Thats the only song though.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Thu Aug 26, 2004 5:13 pm LoL. I knew I was gonna see this topic show up eventually. Lesse, go ahead and kill me now for mentioning a lot of Marilyn Manson songs: "Little Horn" (Shinji), "Cryptorchild" (Shinji), "Wormboy" (Shinji), "Antichrist Superstar" (Unit-01 and Third Impact), "The Reflecting God" (Third Impact), "Man That You Fear" (Vague interpretation of Shinji at Third Impact), "Mechanical Animals" (Rei), "Disassociative" (Shinji), "The Last Day On Earth" (Any readable romantic aspect/I don't know), "Astonishing Panorama of the Endtimes" (Shinji/the series as a series), "Disposable Teens" (Shinji and Gendo), "Crucifiction In Space" (the story as a whole), "The Death Song" (adults), "Count To Six And Die" (the story). And no, I didn't list those for the sake of listing them. "It Doesn't Matter Two" - Depeche Mode (Misato and Kaji), "Mutter" - Rammstein (Asuka, according to manga), "Sparks" - Overseer (Shinji and Asuka), "The Day The Whole World Went Away" - Nine Inch Nails (Post Third Impact), "Closer" - Nine Inch Nails (an event which has not yet transpired), "Sleep Around" - Prince (Misato, though I don't know why; more of a song she'd listen to than a song about her, and one of my very favorite songs partly because of this odd connection)... um... "Ruiner" - Nine Inch Nails (Partly Unit-01, partly some whore character that does not exist; it's more of a sound thing), "Something to Believe In" - Ramones cover by The Pretenders (Shinji, maybe Asuka), "My Gift To You" - KoRn (Shinji, Asuka, and that bizarre strangling ordeal), "No One's There" - KoRn (Shinji; T.V. ending), "I Hate This" - Static-X (Shinji's arrival into the story)... This is actually a pointless exercise, since I could probably find a way to tie every song I have to Eva through some vague emotional interpretation or another.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Aug 26, 2004 6:52 pm Karma Burn wrote: Could someone be gracious enough to post the soundtrack list? As in what manga volume and what scene goes with each song.

[]:             Quote Post by MongolSquad » Thu Aug 26, 2004 9:20 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: I think I'll stick with Sagisu Shiro's music, even if I don't really like it all that much.

[]:             Quote Post by houtaru » Fri Aug 27, 2004 2:19 am I love the present score of evangelion too, especially EOE. Komm, Sussuer Todd is one of my favorite songs of all time. Bethoven's 9th, Air II, and Kanon in D always remind me of Eva. I'll hear Kanon during a wedding or at chruch and think of the characters in Eva playing it. Singles that remind me of Eva are "I'm Just a Kid" - Simple Plan, "In this Together" - Nine Inch Nails, "Gotta Knock a Little Harder" - Mai Yamane. That last one is a Cowboy Bebop song, but it reminds me of Eva too. "The Scientist" - Coldplay, actually reminds me of Gendo and Yui.

[]:             Quote Post by wildarmsheero » Fri Aug 27, 2004 8:14 am houtaru wrote: "Gotta Knock a Little Harder" - Mai Yamane.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Fri Aug 27, 2004 1:15 pm http://www.evamonkey.com/alternate_eva_soundtrack.htm

[]:             Quote Post by Karma Burn » Fri Aug 27, 2004 3:35 pm I think most of those were chosen for their title and not their content...that just pisses me off...

[]: I know what you mean...             Quote Post by Olin of Xephon » Sat Aug 28, 2004 12:07 am I agree Karma Burn, the list just didn't work in my mind, I like most of the songs, just not in relation to music. Although, like the Evamonkey said, it depends on your personal taste. And I didn't start the thread thinking you could come up with something to replace Eva's soundtrack, that would NOT be cool. I was just interested in hearing what songs you assosiate with Evangelion and why? Weezer's 'Tired of Sex' for instance... is there something in Eva that really makes you think of that song or visa versa? Hexon, Weird thing...I didn't think about it before but a lot of Manson songs actually fit...hmm. My personal list... "3 1/2" by AFI With lyrics like "I'm so sick so sick of myslef, mother say you'll pray for me!" and "A small voice in the back of my mind that's whispering words I never wanted to hear.." This song makes me think of Shinji Ikari every time I put it on. "Points of Authority" by Linkin Park. "You love the things I say I'll do- The way I hurt myself again just to get back at you. You take away when I give in, my life, mt pride is broken." Mostly this verse but the whole song is Asuka. "When the world ends" by Dave Mathews Band Puts me in mind of Misato and Kaji. "Death of Seasons" by AFI Aggressive, Beautiful, but Tortured, this song just makes me think of Evangelion in General. "Wide Eyed Wonder girl" by The Choir "Wide eyed wonder girl, staring into the sky and wondering why..." How can you hear that and not instantly see a mental image of everybody's favorite albino, Rei Ayanami? "The great Dissapointment" by AFI Oh, great third A Fire Inside, oh well... It's a really sad song about losing something very important. Just listen to the words, if Gendo had a theme song... "So Far Away" by StainD This makes me think of Shinji's core dillema during the TV ending. "Reality" by StainD This makes me think of Shinji's exteded "therapy session" in End of Eva. "Remember" by Disturbed If Asuka's thoughts while she was being mind raped by the Angel where put into song, it would sound something like this. When the song mentions "a mask" think "the Evangelion" "Darkness" by Disturbed What was going through Misato's mind as she said her final goodbye to Shinji in End Of Evangelion. Pillows music like "another morning" and "Patricia" would go very well with the goofier scenes in Eva. Whenever I think Pillows I think FLCL, of course then I think Eva.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Sat Aug 28, 2004 12:29 am Oh yeah, I forgot "Listen to my Voice," "Pure" and "I Can't Breathe" by Gary Numan. I bought that album when I bought Evangelion, and the two have been married in my mind ever since. If you interpret Evangelion in its occult and emotional aspects and omit its cultural anime aspect, Manson's work is actually interpreting the same dillemas that Evangelion is, and in a very narrative sense. At least that's how I see it. The Antichrist/Omega/ADAM character strongly resemble Shinji to me, or at lest a variation thereof. Though to be honest, ADAM reminds me more of Gendo, or what Shinji might grow up to be. If Antichrist Superstar is loosely telling the same story as Evangelion (and it's hard not to see it unless you absolutely abhor Manson), then Mechanical Animals might present some inpiration for the world that awaits our characters thereafter, and Holy Wood may communicate the kind of person Shinji will grow up to be. Indeed, a sequel may not be impossible. There are a lot of emotional problems to iron out after an ordal like Third Impact. In MA, the people handled it with drugs and distractive shallowness, and a gospel of war had to be preached once more in the spirit of fignting the repetition of mistakes in HW. Shinji may yet have more work to do as a Messiah. Damn, now I want to see those sequels. I smell a pair of long fanfictions...

[]:             Quote Post by Dark FireStar » Tue Sep 14, 2004 6:36 pm Any likin park song goes with eva and Unforgiven 2 by metellica for the impacts

[]:             Quote Post by thedarkorb » Fri Sep 21, 2007 10:14 pm Some nice songs I see hereâ€¦Pretty much any NIN song can be associated with Eva. :P Thought this thread was kind of old and needed some sort of rejuvenating so alasâ€¦I have created my own â€œEva-Mixâ€. Most of the lists and AMVs for Eva that Iâ€™ve seen are very much rock based (70â€™s prog, Fallout Boy, emo crap). There is nothing wrong with rock music, but Iâ€™m starting to find it a little clichÃ© when thrown on top of Eva. So instead of making a simple list, Iâ€™ve decided to do something different and actually create a mix (that you can actually hear!), set to minimal electronic music with a some orchestral stuff thrown in. The text in bold connects the song with a particular character/idea. 01. Intro (The Beast) â€“ Shiro Sagisu 02. JAL To Tokyo â€“ Underworld 03. Idioteque â€“ Radiohead Third Impact 04. Furious Angels (Instrumental) â€“ Rob Dougan The Angels 05. Ave Maria - Giulio Caccini 06. For Silence â€“ Paul Hartnoll Rei-II 07. Iâ€™m Still Awake â€“ Hybrid Shinji 08. 5 Years â€“ BjÃ¶rk Asuka to Shinji 09. Unfinished Sympathy â€“ Massive Attack Misato 10. Overtures: Atmospheres - Gyorgy Ligeti 11. Untitled â€“ Aphex Twin Mind Train/Depression 12. Heart Of Darkness â€“ Elite Force Shinji/EVA-01 13. God Given â€“ Nine Inch Nails Seele 14. Bereit (Ready) â€“ KMFDM Asuka/EVA-02 15. Meltdown â€“ Orbital 16. Domain â€“ The Future Sound Of London 17. Out From The Deep â€“ Enigma Kaworu ACTIVATE: Eva-Redux I understand moderators might have a problem with me posting a link to music that you can download like anywhere else, but let me at least say this. If you like a song in my list, just buy it. Simple as that. Iâ€™ve checked and most of them can be bought via the iTunes music store (that is, if you dig Apple.co). I have posted this here for you guys solely, so donâ€™t even think about linking this to another site! ;) If I get away with posting this, then Iâ€™d like to hear any compilations anyone else has made.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Fri Sep 21, 2007 10:19 pm Necro thread alert. Seriously, this thing hasn't been posted in for 3 years.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Fri Sep 21, 2007 10:24 pm But I thought necromancy was encouraged on this board. Anyway back on topic, Pink Floyd is a damn fine choice to go with Eva IMO.

[]:             Quote Post by thedarkorb » Fri Sep 21, 2007 10:26 pm ...and? I couldn't find any other thread like this and didn't see any need creating the same topic over again.
